**Resolves #12378**

Sub of epic #12377 (GitLab-PAT Bearer auth + MCP Inspector 0.21.2 compat). Implements the GitLab-PAT Bearer auth-mode foundation ‚Äî the contract that is the start-signal for #12379.

**FAIR-band:** under-target [2/30] ‚Äî Self-Selection Rule 1 fires (under-band ‚Üí bias toward author lane). Epic-sub adding a new MCP auth mode + the config-propagation tooling to ship it; additive (OIDC stays the default, untouched).

**Evidence:** L1 (static ‚Äî V-B-A of `AuthService.mjs`, `RequestContextService.mjs`, `BaseConfig.mjs`, the live `auth` block, `initServerConfigs.mjs`) + L2 (dynamic ‚Äî verifier unit spec **plus the consumed `requireBearerAuth` middleware boundary** [valid PAT ‚Üí `next()`/`req.auth`; missing/invalid ‚Üí naked-401] = 8/8, and the drift-detector spec 20/20). The live end-to-end falsification is #12379's lane against the merged endpoint.

## What + Why

MCP servers only supported OIDC bearer auth, which rejects raw Personal Access Tokens (PATs carry no `aud` claim) and advertises OAuth Protected-Resource-Metadata that triggers MCP Inspector's Dynamic Client Registration against providers (like GitLab) that have none. This adds a **config-gated `gitlab-pat` auth mode**: a real user or headless agent authenticates with a GitLab PAT supplied via an env var, validated against the GitLab API ‚Äî no cookie, no OAuth dance.

## Deltas

- **`ai/config.template.mjs`** ‚Äî new realm-root `auth` leaves: `mode` (`NEO_AUTH_MODE`, default `'oidc'`), `gitlabApiBaseUrl` (`NEO_AUTH_GITLAB_API_BASE_URL`, default `https://gitlab.com`), `patCacheTtlSeconds` (`NEO_AUTH_PAT_CACHE_TTL_SECONDS`, default `300`). OIDC remains the production default.
- **`AuthService.mjs`** ‚Äî `setup()` early-returns to a new `setupGitlabPat()` when `mode === 'gitlab-pat'`, installing **only** `requireBearerAuth({verifier})` ‚Äî no `mcpAuthMetadataRouter`, no `resourceMetadataUrl`. A missing/invalid token therefore yields a bare `WWW-Authenticate: Bearer` 401 with no `resource_metadata` breadcrumb (no PRM ‚Üí no Inspector DCR). New `createGitlabPatVerifier()` validates the PAT via `GET {gitlabApiBaseUrl}/api/v4/user`, maps `username ‚Üí userId` / `name || username ‚Üí username` / `source: 'gitlab-pat'` (consumed unchanged by `RequestContextService`), and returns a **numeric `expiresAt`** ‚Äî REQUIRED by `requireBearerAuth` (it rejects auth info lacking one with `Token has no expiration time` before `req.auth` is set); set to the re-validation horizon (`now + patCacheTtlSeconds`, Unix seconds) and built per-call so it stays request-fresh on cache hits. No `aud`/introspection; SHA-256 token-hash success cache (TTL = `patCacheTtlSeconds`; failures never cached; raw token never logged).
- **`TransportService.mjs`** ‚Äî widened the `AuthService.setup()` invocation gate to also fire on `mode === 'gitlab-pat'`. It keyed only on `host`/`issuerUrl`, neither of which PAT mode sets, so PAT mode would otherwise have been silently inert.
- **`initServerConfigs.mjs`** ‚Äî extended the config-template drift detector to project **env-var leaf literals** (`projectSourceShape` ‚Üí `envVars`; `detectDrift` ‚Üí `missingEnvVars`; the materialize-only fast path is disqualified by env drift; both warn loops emit `+ env:`). Without this, a new template `data` leaf (like `NEO_AUTH_MODE`) is invisible to the import/export-only detector, so existing operator configs would never be prompted to migrate ‚Äî leaving PAT mode unreachable. This closes that propagation gap.

## MCP config-template change

This PR adds leaves to `ai/config.template.mjs` (the tracked Tier-1 SSOT). Per the config model, the gitignored per-server / Tier-1 `config.mjs` overlays are autogenerated from templates: a **fresh** deployment clones the updated template (gets PAT mode automatically), while **existing** deployments are now *prompted* by the extended drift detector to run `npm run prepare -- --migrate-config` to pick up the new leaves. No gitignored config files are modified by this PR.

## Test Evidence

- `test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/shared/AuthService.spec.mjs` (new) ‚Äî GitLab-PAT verifier (identity mapping, `name`-absent fallback, `InvalidTokenError` on non-OK with no failure caching, per-token success cache, TTL=0 boundary) **plus the consumed-middleware boundary** (real `requireBearerAuth` installed via `setupGitlabPat`: valid PAT ‚Üí `next()` + `req.auth` populated with numeric `expiresAt`; missing token ‚Üí naked-401 with no `resource_metadata`; invalid PAT ‚Üí 401). **8/8 green.**
- `test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/setup/initServerConfigs.spec.mjs` (+4) ‚Äî env-var projection, `detectDrift` `missingEnvVars`, the env-drift warn path, and env-drift forcing a full migrate. Full spec **20/20 green** (no regression).
- Run: `UNIT_TEST_MODE=true npx playwright test -c test/playwright/playwright.config.unit.mjs`.
- Syntax + whitespace clean; pre-commit hooks (whitespace / shorthand / ticket-archaeology) pass.

## Post-Merge Validation

- [ ] On a fresh deployment generated from the updated template, set `NEO_AUTH_MODE=gitlab-pat` + `NEO_AUTH_GITLAB_API_BASE_URL` and confirm a `read_user` PAT authenticates to `/mcp` (200) while a missing/invalid token gets a bare 401 with no `resource_metadata`.
- [ ] On an existing deployment, confirm `npm run prepare` warns that the configs lack the new `NEO_AUTH_*` leaves and `--migrate-config` propagates them.
- [ ] #12379 live falsification: MCP Inspector 0.21.2 manual-Bearer against the deployed `gitlab-pat` endpoint.

Refs #12377, #12379. Related #12382.
